title: Sept 2024 thoughts — alt-financing real-estate platform diligence (Subject2, Investorlift)
html:
Sept 2024 thoughts — alt-financing real-estate platform diligence (Subject2, Investorlift)

Source: 2024-09-28 Apple Note. Brief diligence questions on a real-estate alternative-financing platform idea — likely something Yahir saw pitched in Shanghai (mid-China-chapter) and was sanity-checking.

TL;DR

  • 6 due-diligence questions on a marketplace concept connecting buyers to alt-financing forms in real estate:
    1. Is the platform connecting or actually doing the financing?
    2. Target wholesalers as the niche?
    3. Is the model job-listing-style — seller posts, buyer applies, seller decides?
    4. Listing-info shape — Zillow + financing layer?
    5. Does this remove deal-scavenging? Just filter searches?
    6. For rollout, choosing one place to run deep is best — potential places?
  • Two reference platforms mentioned: Subject2 and Investorlift.
  • Note ends without a verdict — Yahir doesn't return to this idea in the visible corpus.

Pattern observed

This is Yahir-as-due-diligence-analyst, deploying the same question-laddering approach as his Jensen Q&A prep and the AI search competitor diligence. He doesn't seem to be the founder here — sounds like he's evaluating someone else's pitch (possibly for a friend or as an exercise).

The question structure (target-niche → mechanism → comparison-to-known-thing → unique-value → rollout-strategy) is the same template he uses for Rankett in 2025 12 19 rankett 12 month business plan and 2026 04 27 rankett com. Worth noting as evidence that Yahir's evaluation framework was stable well before Rankett existed.

Open questions

  • Who pitched this? (Note doesn't say — possibly someone in the Shanghai program or a Corvallis connection.)
  • Why no follow-up?

Related

  • subject2
  • investorlift
  • due diligence template
  • rankett — same evaluation framework
